examples.title: Queue Operations - Array Implementation
examples.description: Basic queue operations using array with front and rear pointers
examples.code:
// Queue using Array - Pseudocode
MAXSIZE = 10
queue[10]
front = -1
rear = -1

PROCEDURE isEmpty()
    IF front == -1 OR front > rear THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    ENDIF

PROCEDURE isFull()
    IF rear == MAXSIZE - 1 THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    ENDIF

PROCEDURE enqueue(element)
    IF isFull() THEN
        PRINT "Queue Overflow"
        RETURN
    ENDIF
    IF front == -1 THEN
        front = 0
    ENDIF
    rear = rear + 1
    queue[rear] = element
    PRINT "Enqueued: " + element

PROCEDURE dequeue()
    IF isEmpty() THEN
        PRINT "Queue Underflow"
        RETURN NULL
    ENDIF
    element = queue[front]
    front = front + 1
    IF front > rear THEN
        front = -1
        rear = -1
    ENDIF
    RETURN element
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates array-based queue implementation with isEmpty, isFull, enqueue and dequeue operations. Front pointer tracks removal point, rear tracks insertion point.

examples.title: Circular Queue in C
examples.description: Efficient circular queue implementation using modulo operator
examples.explanation: This C program implements circular queue using modulo operator for wrapping. The key difference from linear queue is that rear and front both wrap to 0 when they reach MAXSIZE-1, allowing space reuse.

examples.title: Queue using Linked List in Python
examples.description: Dynamic queue implementation using Python linked list
examples.explanation: This Python code implements queue using linked list. Enqueue adds at rear (tail), dequeue removes from front (head). Dynamic size - no capacity limit, no memory wastage.

examples.title: BFS using Queue
examples.description: Breadth-First Search algorithm demonstrating queue application
examples.explanation: BFS uses queue to explore vertices level by level. Start vertex is enqueued, then all its unvisited neighbors are enqueued. This ensures vertices are visited in order of their distance from source.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(V) for visited array and queue
